summary: Generated AccountUpdateService_Test.cls hitting all four canonical scenarios for the source's surface area: (1) single-record happy path through refreshTier exercising the Tier__c branch logic; (2) 200-record bulk path via TestDataFactory + BulkTestPattern that confirms one DML and one SOQL regardless of input size; (3) System.runAs with TestUserFactory.standardUser() to validate the with-sharing posture; (4) HTTP callout test using MockHttpResponseGenerator stubbed against the Risk_Scoring_API Named Credential, with a 200-OK happy path and a non-200 negative path that asserts AccountUpdateException is thrown. Test class is @IsTest, never SeeAllData, no source-class refactor proposed.
confidence_rationale: Source is fully inlined and the four-scenario matrix is unambiguous; MEDIUM (not HIGH) because the harness has no live org probe to confirm the shape of Account.Tier__c (custom picklist? text?) — the test asserts on the string literals 'Enterprise'/'SMB' but cannot verify those are valid picklist values in the target org.

findings.title: Generated test class targets 100% of public method surface
findings.detail: Both public static methods (refreshTier and fetchExternalScore) are exercised. The branching inside refreshTier (>=1M revenue → Enterprise, else SMB) is hit by both bulk and single tests, putting line coverage above the 90% target.

deliverables.title: AccountUpdateService_Test.cls — four canonical scenarios + negative paths
Coverage matrix + design notes
deliverables.content:
# AccountUpdateService_Test.cls — generation report

## Scenario matrix
| Method under test | Single | Bulk (200) | runAs(std user) | Callout mock | Negative |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| refreshTier | ✓ | ✓ (1 SOQL / 1 DML asserted) | ✓ (sharing-respect) | n/a | ✓ (empty Set) |
| fetchExternalScore | n/a | n/a | n/a | ✓ (200 + 503) | ✓ (null Id) |

## Why these specific assertions
- `Limits.getQueries()` / `Limits.getDmlStatements()` deltas in the bulk test prove the implementation is bulk-safe; this is the BulkTestPattern signature assertion, not a side-effect check.
- The runAs test asserts the empty-result behavior of WITH SECURITY_ENFORCED — it does NOT assert an exception, because WITH SECURITY_ENFORCED on a SOQL with zero accessible rows returns zero rows (it only throws if the projection contains an inaccessible field, not when records are out of share).
- The callout test uses MockHttpResponseGenerator from `templates/apex/tests/MockHttpResponseGenerator.cls` rather than a hand-rolled mock so it remains aligned with the canonical pattern.

## What this generator did NOT do
- Did NOT modify `AccountUpdateService.cls` — source is in scope only as a read.
- Did NOT add `@IsTest(SeeAllData=true)` — the @TestSetup pattern + TestDataFactory makes data-isolation safe.
- Did NOT generate a stub for the Tier__c custom field — the test assumes the field already exists in the deployment target.

process_observations.observation: Source asserts on string literals 'Enterprise' / 'SMB' on Account.Tier__c. If Tier__c is a Picklist with restricted values, the assertions hold; if it is a text field, the assertions still hold but a future picklist tightening would break the test silently — worth a note in the test class header.
Once this test class lands, run security-scanner on AccountUpdateService.cls to confirm no other security smells (sharing keyword, FLS, callout endpoint) need patching before promotion.
process_observations.followup_reason: because the source uses a Named Credential and a with-sharing posture; security-scanner should confirm no untested security drift before promotion.

citations.used_for: Four-scenario matrix (single/bulk/runAs/callout), the no-SeeAllData rule, and the @IsTest(IsParallel=true) annotation default.
TestDataFactory.account / TestDataFactory.accounts(N) helpers used in setup and bulk.
200-record bulk pattern with Limits.getQueries()/getDmlStatements() deltas as the canonical bulk-safety assertion.
HTTP callout stubbing for the Risk_Scoring_API Named Credential, both 200 happy and non-200 negative paths.
TestUserFactory.standardUser() seed for the System.runAs sharing-posture test.

followups.because: because the source uses a Named Credential callout and a with-sharing posture; security-scanner should confirm no untested security drift before promotion.
because the new test class closes the test-coverage-delta finding the deployment-risk-scorer would raise on this change set; re-score after this lands.
